LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT DISCLOSED AT THE HEARING THAT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL CHARLES GEERHART IS A PRO TEM JUDGE HANDLING DISCOVERY MATTERS. THIS DOES NOT AFFECT JUDGE MILLER'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PLAINTIFF DEIDRENNE ASHCRAFT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR ANY PARTY SEEKING A MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE IN DEPT. 206. MR.PAOLI WITHDREW THEIR REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD THE LOSS OF EARNING CAPACITY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EVENT MAGIC, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 3, 2014 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JANUARY 14, 2014, AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT EVENT MAGIC, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUE 1 (THE PREMISES LIABILITY CAUSE OF ACTION). THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES TWO AND THREE. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT'S LIGHT TREE CAUSED PLAINTIFF'S INJURY. THE TRIER OF FACT WILL DETERMINE HOW THE LIGHT TREE FELL OVER. IN ADDITION, THE COURT DENIES AS TO ISSUE 3 BECAUSE (1) IT APPEARS TO BE AN ATTEMPT TO ADJUDICATE A FACTUAL ISSUE UNDER THE GUISE OF DUTY IN CONTRAVENTION OF CCP 437C(F)(1); AND (2) DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. THE COURT ADMITS THE DECLARATION OF NICHOLE WEST AND DEFENDANT FAILS TO PRESENT SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WILLFULLY CONCEALED THE WITNESS. SEE BILES V. EXXON MOBIL CORP., (2004) 124 CAL.APP.4TH 1315, 1324-1325. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Off Calendar Apr-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicole N. Hancock, Esquire of Shea & Shea, (408)292-2434 appearing for Plaintiff Deidrenne Ashcraft.
G. Dennis Rodgers, Esquire of Creswell Echeguren Rodgers & Harvey, (510)444-1735 appearing for Defendant Prive Bar Lounge.
Juliet MacMillin Lompa, Esquire of Stone & Associates, ((925)938-1555 appearing for Defendant Event Magic, Inc.

The parties stipulate to the matter being decided by Judge Pro Tem James Watson. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: DEFENDANTS Prive Bar Lounge and Event Magic's Motion To Exclude Witness Nichole West And To Exclude Evidence Of Plaintiff's New Injury Claim Developed With Assistance Of Counsel After The Close Of Discovery And After Disclosure Of Expert Witnesses, Or In The Alternative, For An Order Allowing Defendants To Conduct Discovery Of This New Evidence Only is DENIED without Prejudice. Order is signed in open Court. Judge Pro Tem: James Watson, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-20-2014 Continued To Jun-20-14 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 212

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NICOLE N. HANCOCK OF SHEA & SHEA LAW OFFICES, TEL. NO. (408) 292-2434 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JULIET MACMILLIN LOMPA OF STONE & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. (925) 938-1555 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT EVENT MAGIC, INCORPORATED;
ATTY. G. DENNIS RODGERS OF THE LAW FIRM OF CRESSELL, ECHENGUREN RODGERS & HARVEY, TEL. NO. (510) 444-1735 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS ROE ET. AL., BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EVENT MAGIC, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication - The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 1 (the premises liability cause of action). The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues two and three. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's light tree caused plaintiff's injury. The trier of fact will determine how the light tree fell over. In addition, the Court denies as to issue 3 because (1) it appears to be an attempt to adjudicate a factual issue under the guise of duty in contravention of CCP 437c(f)(1); and (2) defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The Court admits the declaration of Nichole West and defendant fails to present substantial evidence that plaintiff willfully concealed the witness. See Biles v. Exxon Mobil Corp., (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 1315, 1324-1325. The Court denies defendant's request for a continuance. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/AR

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Continued To Feb-03-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EVENT MAGIC, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is Continued to February 3, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 14, 2014, at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
